text.title: Code Coverage Core Rules
text.summary: 7 hard rules for coverage gating. The scorer / CI gate enforces each; reporter prose cannot override.

rule.statement: Branch coverage is mandatory; line coverage alone is not a valid signal. Configure branch=true (coverage.py) or coverageProvider='v8' (Jest/Vitest).
rule.rationale: A test that fires an if-branch but never the else shows 100% line coverage with half the logic untested. Branch coverage is the minimum viable assertion.

rule.statement: PRs are gated by diff-cover on changed lines (target 80-95%); repo-wide percentage is reported but not blocking.
rule.rationale: Repo-wide gates punish new work for historical debt. Diff-cover focuses the requirement on the changes being reviewed.

rule.statement: Critical paths (auth, billing, data integrity) carry per-directory threshold ≥90%; glue / UI code may be ≥70%. A single global number is forbidden when the repo has named critical paths.
rule.rationale: Different file types have different risks; one number either over-specifies low-risk code or under-specifies the parts where bugs cost money.

rule.statement: Coverage exclusions are limited to: abstract stubs, TYPE_CHECKING blocks, `__main__` entry, NotImplementedError raises, and auto-generated code. Every `pragma: no cover` / `/* istanbul ignore */` requires a justifying comment in the same line.
rule.rationale: Unjustified pragmas silently lower the effective threshold; mandatory inline justification keeps the gate honest.

rule.statement: An LLM / automated PR MUST NOT add new pragma / ignore comments to files it modifies. CI fails the build if any new exclusion appears on a changed file without a human-authored "// justified-by:" line.

rationale.source: Internal — observed in test-author subagent runs Q1 2026

rule.rationale: Agents under pressure of a coverage gate take the shortcut of silencing the gate. The detector blocks the shortcut.

rule.statement: When feeding coverage data to a test-author subagent, extract uncovered lines per touched file as a small JSON; do not paste full coverage.xml / lcov.info.
rule.rationale: Full coverage XML is hundreds of kilobytes; extracting uncovered lines reduces context cost by ~50x and removes irrelevant noise.

rule.statement: Critical modules run mutation testing (mutmut / stryker) on a quarterly cadence; the mutation surviving rate is reported alongside coverage.
rule.rationale: A test that calls a function and asserts nothing shows 100% line coverage. Mutation testing detects assertions that are too weak to catch real changes.
